channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: 15 More Disney Games Have Been Delisted on Steam
item.content:encoded:
A number of Disney games from across the media giant's back catalog have been suddenly delisted from Steam, and no reason has been provided as to why they've been removed.

As spotted by Wario64 on X, 15 Disney games have been taken off the digital storefront, so they're no longer available to purchase if you didn't already buy them. Delisted games can usually still be played even if they're not on the store anymore, though, so make sure to check if you own one of the games in question.

Disney hasn't made any kind of official statement regarding the delisting of these games, so we don't know why they've been removed. Rights reasons are often to blame for this sort of thing, but these are all Disney-published games, so the situation seems a little more complex than that.

Here's the full list of Disney games being removed from Steam as part of this delisting round.

  • Disney High School Musical 3: Senior Year Dance
  • Disney+Pixar Brave: The Video Game
  • Bolt
  • Disney's Treasure Planet: Battle of Procyon
  • Disney Alice in Wonderland
  • Chicken Little
  • Disney Tangled
  • Disney G-Force
  • Disney Universe
  • Disney Princess: My Fairytale Adventure
  • Disney Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End
  • Planet of the Apes: Last Frontier
  • Star Wars Rebellion
  • Star Wars Dark Forces (Classic)
  • Outlaws + A Handful of Missions (Classic)

It's worth doing a little housekeeping here. The versions of Dark Forces and Outlaws being delisted are the classic versions, not the more recent remasters of both titles.

Additionally, Planet of the Apes: Last Frontier wasn't released under Disney's banner, but under that of 20th Century Fox, which Disney bought after the game was released. That one's quite a bit more recent than the others, too.

Back in 2024, Disney coughed up a whopping $1.5 billion to invest in Epic Games and create "a transformational new games and entertainment universe" within the latter's battle royale game Fortnite.

However, reports currently claim that Disney and Epic are working together on a Marathon or Arc Raiders-style extraction shooter. Fortnite's fortunes appear to be on the downturn right now, so making standalone games makes more sense.

What this could mean, though, is that Disney is jumping ship away from Steam and towards the Epic Games Store. Even with that in mind, I wouldn't expect to see a lot of these games resurface, unless it's through GOG's Preservation Program.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
